description:
This is a false-colour infrared satellite image depicting an urban and peri-urban landscape. Vegetation is highlighted in shades of red, with brighter reds indicating vigorous vegetation such as dense tree cover or actively growing green spaces, and darker reds or maroon indicating sparser vegetation. Artificial surfaces, including roads, buildings, and other impervious areas, appear in cool tones of grey, light blue, and cyan. Bare ground or dry soil appears in brownish-grey.

The image displays a highly structured urban area with a prominent grid-like street network and at least six large roundabouts functioning as key intersections. Multiple buildings, visible as light blue/cyan rectangular structures, are clustered in the upper right quadrant, suggesting a residential or commercial district. A major multi-lane highway or road runs diagonally from the upper left to the lower central part of the image, flanked by a large parking area to its west and an elongated, narrow water feature (likely a river or canal) further to the west. Extensive green spaces are integrated throughout the area, notably a large park-like setting in the lower right with winding paths and distinctly shaped vegetated zones. Undeveloped plots with bare ground or sparse vegetation are visible in the central and lower-left portions of the image. No scale bar, compass, legend, or specific location labels are visible.
